metadata.title: The Smaller Model That Was Eighty Times Slower

beats.narration_text: Annyeong, Liam. This is Liam, in for Bear. The LSTM had fewer parameters than the CNN — and ran nearly eighty times slower.

props.command: Why is my LSTM slower than a CNN that has seven times more parameters?
output: LSTM: 14M MACs/step × 100 steps = 1.4B total. CNN: 17M MACs total. 82× difference.

beats.narration_text: The book's memory map: weights live in flash, activations fight for SRAM. For an LSTM every time-step reuses weights but allocates new hidden-state activations.
The CNN processes the entire input in one pass: 17 million multiply-accumulate operations, done. The counter fills once and stops. Parameters: 1.4 million. Inference cost: fixed, predictable, fast.

The LSTM runs its full computation once per time step. 14 million MACs at step 1. Then again at step 2. Then step 3. Times 100 steps: 1.4 billion total multiply-accumulate operations. The counter climbs with every box that unfolds. Parameters: 0.2 million — the LSTM is the smaller model. Operations: 82 times more.

Here is the inversion. Left column: parameters. LSTM has 0.2 million — smaller. CNN has 1.4 million — seven times larger. Right column: total ops. LSTM runs 1.4 billion. CNN runs 17 million — the 80× difference. Parameter count measures storage. Ops count measures time. Choose your model by the number that matters for your deadline.

Your turn. Paste this into Claude: I have a time-series model choice — LSTM with units hidden units, or a 1D CNN with filters filters. My sequence length is T timesteps. Walk me through: one, LSTM MACs per step and total; two, CNN MACs total; three, how to decide which fits my latency-budget millisecond budget; four, what GRU would cost by comparison. Swap in your architecture numbers and latency target.

props.command: I have a time-series model choice: LSTM with [units] hidden units, or a 1D CNN with [filters] filters. My sequence length is [T] timesteps. Walk me through: (1) LSTM MACs per step and total, (2) CNN MACs total, (3) how to decide which fits my [latency_budget] ms budget, (4) what GRU would cost by comparison.
